commands: prod: docker build -f ~/guvfx-app/deploy/wayond-listener/Dockerfile --build-arg BACKEND_IMAGE=guvfx-prod-guvfx-backend:latest -t guvfx-wayond-listener:latest (listener = current trading image + telethon)
prod: throwaway run — verify code present + migrate --plan (no operations)
prod: docker rm -f guvfx-wayond-listener; docker run -d ... --env-file wayond-listener.env ... run_wayond_listener --live (same session)
prod: verify code in running container, session reconnect, execution boundary, no dispatch capability
expected_results: The listener image is rebuilt from current main code (via the current trading image) so it contains signal_intake.signals, execution.auto_router (AUTO_DEMO tier), promote_plan_to_demo_jobs, CanonicalTradeResult, TelegramRenderer, the current parser, and the current notification chain.
The listener stays healthy; the Telethon session is preserved (reused wayond-listener.env, no re-auth); the Wayond subscription is intact.
No functional/armed change: provider ONBOARDING, auto_execution_enabled False, mode SHADOW, dispatch OFF; no ExecutionJob created; no NotificationDelivery sent.
The trading image and other services are untouched.
actual_results: Listener rebuilt: new image 36003dc9d849 (from BACKEND_IMAGE=guvfx-prod-guvfx-backend:latest 342d198f9008 + telethon 1.44.0); trading image 342d198f9008 unchanged; stale image tagged guvfx-wayond-listener:rollback-preRedeploy (154806e94bc3).

Running-container code VERIFIED present: signal_intake.signals.signal_acquired=True; execution.auto_router.MODE_AUTO_DEMO=True; promote_plan_to_demo_jobs=True; CanonicalTradeResult=True; TelegramRenderer=True; RealTelegramTransport(notification chain)=True; current parser registry importable.
migrate --plan: 'No planned migration operations' (shared DB already current). No migration applied.
Listener healthy: 'Up (healthy)'; logs show reconnect -> catch-up processed=120 -> heartbeat state=listening chats=1. Telethon SESSION VALID (not de-authed); wayond subscription intact.
Execution boundary CLEAN: ExecutionJob total = 50 (unchanged baseline); Wayond-tagged jobs (WAY comment) = 0; NotificationDelivery total = 0 (transmitted 0); NotificationCandidate = 0.

Acquisition on catch-up: AcquiredMessage outcome = {DROPPED_NOT_ARMED: 120} (un-armed provider drops all); the current code minted 6 PENDING_APPROVAL audit-anchor approvals for parsed signals (the old stale listener minted 0) — none executed; boundary-safe.

Safety defaults unchanged: provider wayond=ONBOARDING; auto_execution_enabled=False; signal_execution_mode=SHADOW. Listener env has NO TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN / NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED / NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_TRANSPORT -> the listener cannot dispatch/send.
limitations: Production deploy scoped to the listener image only. Nothing armed: provider ONBOARDING, auto off, mode SHADOW, dispatch OFF. No order, no trade, no Telegram send. E3 remains RED.

The Telethon session was PRESERVED (reused the unchanged wayond-listener.env; no re-auth attempted) — the de-auth risk was avoided, and the session reconnected healthily. The session remains a live temp-personal-account credential.

BEHAVIOURAL NOTE: the current acquisition code mints a PENDING audit-anchor PendingSignalApproval for each parsed Wayond signal even when un-armed (the old stale listener minted none). These sit PENDING_APPROVAL, execute nothing, and are inert (the auto-router is event-driven and never re-processes existing approvals). They will accumulate while the provider is un-armed; a later armed demo executes only the NEW signal at that moment, unaffected by them. Optional future cleanup, not required.

wayond-listener.env holds a snapshot of the prod DB creds (unchanged since capture) + the Telethon session; a DB-cred rotation (recommended after the earlier inline-compose exposure) would require re-capturing it and restarting the listener.
artefact_locations: prod listener image guvfx-wayond-listener:latest (36003dc9d849, current) — running
